raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/2025, I placed an online order with Best Buy for merchandise totaling {$530.00} ( XXXX XXXX. XXXX ), charged to my My Best Buy Credit Card issued by Citibank , N.A . The order was marked as delivered by the carrier, but I never received the package. On XX/XX/2025, Best Buy canceled the order and confirmed by email that a refund would be processed within XXXX business days. When no refund appeared, I contacted Best Buy on XX/XX/2025 and was informed that the refund was denied based on a delivery photo. The photo provided does not show my residence, displays no house number, and includes no signature or confirmation of receipt. Best Buy then told me I must file a police report to reopen my claim, even though I have no evidence of theft or criminal activityonly a misdelivery by the carrier. 

Believing this charge constitutes a billing error under the Fair Credit Billing Act ( FCBA ), 15 U.S.C. 1666, and Regulation Z, 12 C.F.R. 1026.13, I contacted Citibank on XX/XX/2025 to dispute the charge for goods not delivered as agreed. Citi confirmed on XX/XX/2025 that the {$530.00} charge was placed in dispute, no payment would be due on that portion during investigation, and the review would be completed within 90 days. However, I remain concerned that Citibank may rely on Best Buys unverified delivery photo or police-report demand to deny the refund, even though both are inconsistent with federal and North Carolina consumer protection laws. 

Under N.C. Gen. Stat. 25-2-503, 25-2-601, 25-2-509, and 25-2-510, the risk of loss remains with the seller until the goods are actually received and accepted by the buyer. Since Best Buy canceled the order and promised a refund, it has no right to retain the payment. Conditioning refund reconsideration on a police report also conflicts with N.C. Gen. Stat. 14-225, which prohibits false or unfounded reports to law enforcement. I am requesting that Citibank investigate this FCBA billing error, issue a full credit of {$530.00} to my account, reverse any interest or fees, and ensure no negative credit reporting occurs during or after the dispute period.

This complaint is submitted to the CFPB because Best Buys cancellation and refund promise created a binding obligation to return my payment, and Citibankas the creditorhas a statutory duty to ensure correction of this billing error. I am requesting CFPB review and enforcement of my rights under the Fair Credit Billing Act, Regulation Z, and applicable North Carolina consumer protection statutes.
